danger_signals.visual_form: Orange shadows on right side of combat screen
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates opponent party location and active threat in battle

danger_signals.visual_form: Character corruption (Darius transformation into Void's vessel)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual manifestation of evil god's corrupting power over individuals
Character approaching death; visual feedback of vulnerability
Spellcasters forced to cast weak spells; reduced combat capability

danger_signals.visual_form: Combat initiation during travel (exact visual undefined)
danger_signals.meaning: World state danger communicates through unpredictable threat emergence

safety_signals.visual_form: Blue shadows on left side of combat screen
safety_signals.meaning: Player's assembled team; visual distinction from enemies
Interior spaces provide separation from overworld threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Ring of statues marking portal locations
safety_signals.meaning: Fast travel infrastructure; visual landmark for navigation safety

safety_signals.visual_form: Rest state restoring strength and magic meters
safety_signals.meaning: Designated recovery points communicate strategic safety zones

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Academy location; Guildmaster Thalmus the Wise seat
Represented by party member Obliky; spell-casting ability

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Darius as Void's physical vessel; minion presence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fount of Heaven (final confrontation location); corrupted zones

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Certain tasks and quests only accomplishable during daylight

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lighting shift (implicit from day-night cycle mechanic)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alternative quest paths; night-specific NPCs and encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Journey to Fount of Heaven; palace construction finale
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final confrontation with Darius/Void; world transformation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rings hidden in legend; world appears normal initially
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs unaware of immediate threat; gradual quest revelation

environmental_storytelling.example: Six Guilds (Knights, Conjurors, Archers, Enchanters, Necromancers, Sorcerers) represented by recruited party members; each Art of discipline has visual class distinction
Fount of Heaven as Nexus's seat; Rings of Power hidden across Ushka Bau landscape; statues marking magical gateways communicate ancient divine presence
Vehicle access (dinosaurs, boats, ships, dragons) implies world scale and diverse biomes; gatewaystatue rings suggest pre-existing civilization infrastructure
Darius transformation into Void's vessel shows literal visual corruption; student-turned-antagonist demonstrates Void's corrupting influence through character model changes
Blue shadows (player left side) vs. orange shadows (enemy right side) creates visual faction separation; spatial layout reinforces alliance vs. opposition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Leads assembled team; initiates party formation; makes strategic decisions
Attacked and killed by corrupted student; dying act teleports party (shows mentor sacrifice pattern)
Assassination of Guildmaster; transformation into Void's physical vessel; final boss confrontation

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Party members (Slash, Mortimer, Feather, Alexi, Obliky)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Display class-specific spell abilities; respond to player combat strategy adjustments (aggression levels)
Conversation-initiation for Ring clues; susceptible to bribery with currency; some fall under Void's corruption
Random combat encounters; oppose party advancement; attempt Rod reassembly

notes: Rings of Power uses isometric perspective with diagonal control scheme, positioning visual communication through screen-space faction placement (blue/orange shadow distinction). World state communicates through: (1) day-night cycle gating quest availability, (2) Ring collection progress as meta-world-state, (3) NPC corruption as Void influence manifestation, (4) transported party members at game start indicating world-disruption. Faction identity relies on party composition (six disciplines) rather than heavy color/sigil coding—identity communicates through class-specific spell visuals and equipped characters. Environmental storytelling emphasizes: sacred locations (Fount of Heaven), guild structure visualization, corruption mechanics, and transportation network diversity. Danger signals primarily manifest through combat mechanics (meter depletion) and encounter randomization. T3 data tier limits granular visual documentation; many exact visual assets remain undescribed in available source.
